✅ DEFRA Compliance Checker

Step-by-step compliance health check covering cross compliance, ELM schemes, nutrient management, biosecurity and environmental requirements.

Step 1 of 8
Farm Profile

What best describes your farm type?

Your farm type determines which DEFRA requirements apply to you.
Farm Profile

What is your total farmed area?

Area determines whether you are subject to mandatory ELM participation thresholds and nitrate regulations.
Nutrient Management

Is your farm in a Nitrate Vulnerable Zone (NVZ)?

NVZs have strict rules on when and how much nitrogen fertiliser and slurry you can apply. Check the Gov.uk NVZ checker.
Nutrient Management

Do you have a Nutrient Management Plan (NMP)?

An NMP is mandatory if you are in an NVZ and have livestock. It is required to access the Sustainable Farming Incentive (SFI).
Environmental Land Management

Are you currently enrolled in any ELM scheme?

The Sustainable Farming Incentive (SFI), Countryside Stewardship (CS) and Landscape Recovery are the three ELM tiers replacing BPS payments.
Biosecurity

Do you have a written biosecurity plan?

A biosecurity plan is required under the Animal Health Act and is assessed during Red Tractor audits and APHA inspections.
Record Keeping

Are your farm records up to date?

DEFRA requires records to be kept for at least 5 years for cross-compliance audits. This includes spraying records, livestock movement records and field operations.
Water & Environment

Do you have an abstraction licence if you take water from rivers, boreholes or reservoirs?

Abstracting water without a licence from the Environment Agency is an offence. Small quantities (under 20m³/day) are usually exempt.
Compliance Score: —

Your Farm Compliance Report

Based on your answers — always verify with DEFRA / your local FAS adviser.
